title: PrincipleResolutionRecord
description: Immutable record of a principle arbitration decision produced by AEP-MEM-003. Every principle conflict is logged — the system never silently chooses. The losing principle is subordinated (active set to false in the registry) but preserved as a historical record. The resolution creates a first-class causal link between the two principles and records the justification for the outcome.

resolution_id.description: Content-addressed identifier derived from principle_a, principle_b, conflict_type, and winner. Deterministic and deduplication-safe.

principle_a.description: principle_id of the first participant in the conflict.

principle_b.description: principle_id of the second participant in the conflict.

conflict_type.description: Classification of the conflict that required arbitration. TAXONOMY_OVERRIDE: the two principles sit at different taxonomy hierarchy levels; the higher-authority level wins unconditionally. SPECIFICITY_COLLISION: the two principles sit at the same taxonomy level but cover different scope widths; the narrower (more specific) scope wins. AGGREGATION_LIMIT: no single pairwise conflict, but the combined tolerance contributions of multiple active principles would breach the global invariant envelope; detected by aggregateEnvelopeCheck(), not recorded as a pairwise resolution.

winner.description: principle_id of the principle whose constraint prevails. Remains active in the registry.

loser.description: principle_id of the subordinated principle. Marked inactive in the registry. The principle entry itself is preserved as an immutable historical record.

priority_delta.description: Human-readable explanation of why the winner prevailed. For TAXONOMY_OVERRIDE: records the class and rank of each participant (e.g. 'LEVEL_0_DETERMINISM_INTEGRITY (rank 0) > LEVEL_3_BEHAVIORAL_OPTIMIZATION (rank 3)'). For SPECIFICITY_COLLISION: records the scope descriptor and specificity score of each participant.

simulation_verified.description: True when simulation was used to confirm the resolution outcome. Provides additional assurance beyond structural rule application.
